metadata.curriculum: Cambridge International AS & A Level Computer Science (9618)
metadata.description: A-Level Computer Science topics with prerequisite ordering, colored by component
sources: Cambridge International AS & A Level Computer Science 9618 Syllabus

nodes.label: Number Systems
nodes.description: Binary, denary, and hexadecimal number systems; conversion between bases

nodes.label: Binary Arithmetic
nodes.description: Binary addition, subtraction, overflow; two's complement representation for signed integers

nodes.label: Binary Coded Decimal (BCD)
nodes.description: BCD representation of denary numbers and its applications

nodes.label: Character Encoding
nodes.description: ASCII and Unicode character sets; encoding text data in binary

nodes.label: Image Representation
nodes.description: Bitmap image representation; pixel, resolution, colour depth; image file size calculation

nodes.label: Sound Representation
nodes.description: Analogue-to-digital conversion; sampling rate, sample resolution, bit rate; sound file size calculation

nodes.label: Data Compression
nodes.description: Lossless vs lossy compression; run-length encoding (RLE); need for compression

nodes.label: Network Types & Topologies
nodes.description: LAN, WAN, PAN; bus, star, mesh topologies; client-server vs peer-to-peer models

nodes.label: Transmission Media & Hardware
nodes.description: Wired and wireless transmission; routers, switches, hubs, NIC, WAP; bandwidth and latency

nodes.label: Network Protocols
nodes.description: TCP/IP, HTTP/HTTPS, FTP, POP3, IMAP, SMTP, BitTorrent; packet switching and circuit switching

nodes.label: The Internet & World Wide Web
nodes.description: Structure of the internet; URLs, DNS, ISPs; the difference between the internet and WWW

nodes.label: Input & Output Devices
nodes.description: Types and uses of input devices (keyboard, mouse, scanner, microphone) and output devices (monitor, printer, speakers)

nodes.label: Storage Devices & Media
nodes.description: Primary vs secondary storage; magnetic, optical, solid-state; RAM, ROM; virtual memory

nodes.label: Logic Gates & Logic Circuits
nodes.description: AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR gates; truth tables; constructing and interpreting logic circuits

nodes.label: CPU Architecture
nodes.description: Von Neumann architecture; ALU, control unit, registers (PC, MAR, MDR, CIR, ACC); system buses

nodes.label: Fetch-Decode-Execute Cycle
nodes.description: Stages of the FDE cycle; role of registers and buses during instruction processing

nodes.label: Instruction Sets & Addressing Modes
nodes.description: Machine code instructions; opcode and operand; immediate, direct, indirect, indexed addressing

nodes.label: Assembly Language
nodes.description: Assembly language instructions; mnemonics; writing and tracing simple assembly programs

nodes.label: Operating Systems
nodes.description: Functions of an OS; process management, memory management, I/O management, file management; interrupts

nodes.label: Language Translators
nodes.description: Compilers, interpreters, assemblers; stages of compilation; comparison of translation methods

nodes.label: Utility Software
nodes.description: Disk formatter, defragmenter, backup, virus checker, file compression; role of utility programs

nodes.label: Encryption
nodes.description: Symmetric and asymmetric encryption; public/private keys; SSL/TLS; digital signatures and certificates

nodes.label: Malware & Cyber Threats
nodes.description: Viruses, worms, trojans, spyware, ransomware, phishing, pharming, DoS attacks

nodes.label: Firewalls & Authentication
nodes.description: Firewalls, proxy servers, authentication methods, biometrics, access control, user accounts

nodes.label: Data Integrity & Verification
nodes.description: Parity checks, checksums, check digits, verification vs validation; error detection methods

nodes.label: Privacy & Data Protection
nodes.description: Privacy issues; data protection legislation; cookies; personal data collection and use

nodes.label: Ethics & Computing
nodes.description: Ethical issues in computing; autonomous vehicles, AI ethics, digital surveillance, social media impact

nodes.label: Intellectual Property & Licensing
nodes.description: Copyright, patents, trademarks; software licensing (open source, proprietary, freeware, shareware)

nodes.label: Databases (AS)
nodes.description: Relational database concepts; tables, records, fields, primary keys, foreign keys; single-table and multi-table SQL queries; DDL and DML

nodes.label: Pseudocode & Flowcharts
nodes.description: Writing and interpreting pseudocode; program flowcharts; structured English; trace tables

nodes.label: Decomposition & Abstraction
nodes.description: Problem decomposition into sub-problems; abstraction to remove unnecessary detail; top-down design

nodes.label: Linear Search
nodes.description: Linear search algorithm; implementation in pseudocode; efficiency considerations

nodes.label: Binary Search
nodes.description: Binary search algorithm on sorted data; implementation; comparison with linear search

nodes.label: Bubble Sort
nodes.description: Bubble sort algorithm; implementation in pseudocode; analysis of passes and comparisons

nodes.label: Insertion Sort
nodes.description: Insertion sort algorithm; implementation; comparison with bubble sort

nodes.label: Variables, Constants & Data Types
nodes.description: Declaring variables and constants; integer, real, char, string, Boolean, date data types; type casting

nodes.label: Selection Statements
nodes.description: IF-THEN-ELSE, nested IF, CASE/SWITCH statements; Boolean expressions and conditions

nodes.label: Iteration & Loops
nodes.description: FOR, WHILE, REPEAT-UNTIL loops; count-controlled vs condition-controlled iteration; nested loops

nodes.label: Procedures & Functions
nodes.description: Defining and calling procedures and functions; parameters (by value, by reference); return values; local and global variables

nodes.label: String Operations
nodes.description: String handling: length, substring, concatenation, upper/lower case, character-to-ASCII conversion

nodes.label: File Handling
nodes.description: Reading from and writing to text files; opening, closing, appending; sequential file processing

nodes.label: Arrays
nodes.description: One-dimensional and two-dimensional arrays; declaring, initialising, accessing and iterating through arrays

nodes.label: User-Defined Data Types & Records
nodes.description: Defining record types; enumerated and pointer types; using records to group related data

nodes.label: Stacks
nodes.description: Stack as LIFO structure; push, pop, peek operations; implementing stacks using arrays; applications

nodes.label: Queues
nodes.description: Queue as FIFO structure; enqueue, dequeue operations; linear and circular queue implementations

nodes.label: Linked Lists
nodes.description: Singly linked list structure; inserting, deleting, traversing nodes; comparison with arrays

nodes.label: Program Testing
nodes.description: Types of testing: unit, integration, system, acceptance; test data: normal, boundary, erroneous; dry-run and trace tables

nodes.label: Software Development Lifecycle (Basics)
nodes.description: Stages of the SDLC: analysis, design, coding, testing, maintenance; waterfall model introduction

nodes.label: Floating-Point Representation
nodes.description: Floating-point binary representation; mantissa and exponent; normalisation; precision, range and rounding errors

nodes.label: User-Defined Data Types (A2)
nodes.description: Non-composite types (enumerated, pointer) and composite types (record, set, class/object); type definitions

nodes.label: Protocol Layering & TCP/IP Stack
nodes.description: TCP/IP four-layer model; application, transport, internet, network access layers; benefits of layered approach

nodes.label: Packet Structure & Switching
nodes.description: Packet headers, payload, and trailers; circuit switching vs packet switching; routing algorithms

nodes.label: IP Addressing & Subnetting
nodes.description: IPv4 and IPv6; subnet masks; public and private IP addresses; NAT and DHCP

nodes.label: Client-Server & Web Technologies
nodes.description: Client-server model; web servers; HTML, CSS, JavaScript basics; web browser rendering; APIs

nodes.label: RISC vs CISC Processors
nodes.description: Reduced vs complex instruction set computing; comparison of architectures; design trade-offs

nodes.label: Pipelining & Parallel Processing
nodes.description: Instruction pipelining; multi-core processors; parallel processing; SIMD, MIMD architectures; massively parallel computers

nodes.label: Virtual Machines
nodes.description: Role of virtual machines; benefits and limitations; hypervisors; virtual machine software; bytecode execution

nodes.label: Boolean Algebra
nodes.description: Boolean identities and laws (De Morgan's, distribution, commutation); simplifying Boolean expressions algebraically

nodes.label: Karnaugh Maps
nodes.description: Using Karnaugh maps for 2, 3 and 4 variables to simplify Boolean expressions; don't care conditions

nodes.label: Flip-Flops & Sequential Circuits
nodes.description: SR and JK flip-flops; timing diagrams; sequential logic circuits; memory storage using flip-flops

nodes.label: OS Process Management
nodes.description: Process states and transitions; scheduling algorithms (round robin, priority, shortest job first); interrupts and ISRs

nodes.label: OS Memory Management
nodes.description: Paging, segmentation, virtual memory; page tables; thrashing; memory allocation strategies

nodes.label: Compiler Construction Stages
nodes.description: Lexical analysis, syntax analysis (parsing), code generation and optimisation; BNF and syntax diagrams; reverse Polish notation

nodes.label: BNF & Syntax Diagrams
nodes.description: Backus-Naur Form notation; syntax diagrams; defining and interpreting language grammar rules

nodes.label: Reverse Polish Notation
nodes.description: Converting infix to postfix (RPN); evaluating RPN expressions using a stack; benefits of RPN for compilers

nodes.label: Security (Advanced)
nodes.description: Advanced encryption techniques; hashing; digital certificates; SQL injection; cross-site scripting; social engineering

nodes.label: Monitoring & Control Systems
nodes.description: Sensors, actuators, ADC/DAC; feedback loops; real-time processing; embedded systems; applications in industry

nodes.label: Recursion
nodes.description: Recursive algorithms; base case and general case; call stack; comparison with iteration; tail recursion

nodes.label: Merge Sort
nodes.description: Divide-and-conquer merge sort algorithm; recursive implementation; time complexity analysis

nodes.label: Quick Sort
nodes.description: Quick sort algorithm using pivot selection and partitioning; recursive implementation; best/worst/average case

nodes.label: Big-O Notation & Algorithm Complexity
nodes.description: Time complexity: O(1), O(log n), O(n), O(n log n), O(n^2), O(2^n); space complexity; comparing algorithm efficiency

nodes.label: Binary Trees
nodes.description: Binary tree structure; inserting, searching, deleting nodes; in-order, pre-order, post-order traversal

nodes.label: Hash Tables
nodes.description: Hashing functions; collision resolution (linear probing, chaining); hash table operations; applications

nodes.label: Graphs
nodes.description: Directed and undirected graphs; weighted graphs; adjacency matrix and adjacency list representations

nodes.label: Graph Traversal Algorithms
nodes.description: Depth-first search (DFS) and breadth-first search (BFS); applications; implementation using stacks and queues

nodes.label: Dijkstra's Shortest Path Algorithm
nodes.description: Dijkstra's algorithm for finding shortest paths in weighted graphs; step-by-step execution; applications

nodes.label: Classes & Objects
nodes.description: Defining classes; creating objects; attributes (properties) and methods; constructors and destructors; this/self keyword

nodes.label: Encapsulation & Access Modifiers
nodes.description: Public, private, protected access; getters and setters; information hiding; benefits of encapsulation

nodes.label: Inheritance
nodes.description: Base and derived classes; inheriting attributes and methods; overriding methods; IS-A relationship

nodes.label: Polymorphism
nodes.description: Method overriding and overloading; runtime polymorphism; using polymorphism for flexible code design

nodes.label: Database Normalisation
nodes.description: First, second and third normal forms (1NF, 2NF, 3NF); functional dependencies; entity-relationship diagrams

nodes.label: SQL (Advanced)
nodes.description: Multi-table queries with JOIN; GROUP BY and HAVING; aggregate functions; subqueries; ALTER TABLE; CREATE INDEX

nodes.label: Declarative Programming
nodes.description: Declarative programming paradigm; Prolog-style facts, rules and queries; logic programming concepts

nodes.label: Software Development Methodologies
nodes.description: Waterfall, iterative, agile, RAD, extreme programming; choosing appropriate methodology; prototyping

nodes.label: Testing Strategies (Advanced)
nodes.description: Black-box vs white-box testing; stub testing; integration testing; alpha and beta testing; acceptance testing

nodes.label: Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
nodes.description: Machine learning concepts; neural networks; backpropagation; deep learning; A* algorithm; applications of AI

nodes.label: File Organisation & Access
nodes.description: Serial, sequential, random (direct) and indexed sequential file organisation; hashing for file access

nodes.label: Exception Handling
nodes.description: Try-catch-finally blocks; throwing and catching exceptions; creating custom exception classes; defensive programming
